reviewer.notes: Reviewer read the actual PDF directly. More thorough than the earlier Grok review — found specific issues in the proof text.

claim_reviews.evidence: GPT-5.2 flagged '8863.57' as wrong — this was a misread of 886^{3.57} in the LaTeX. Actual math: 536/0.6046 = 886.5, exponent = 3.571, 886.5^3.571 = 3.37e10. The computation is correct. LaTeX clarified to use \left(\frac{}{} notation to prevent misreading.

claim_reviews.verdict: DISPUTED — valid criticism, proof step was incomplete
claim_reviews.evidence: GPT-5.2 correctly identified that line 304 claimed |H| >= p^2-1 without justification. The correct argument is that H is not contained in any maximal proper subgroup (Borel, Cartan, exceptional) by steps (i)-(iii), so H = SL_2(F_p) by Dickson's theorem. The order bound was the wrong approach. Proof rewritten.
claim_reviews.concerns: This was a real error in the proof. Fixed.

claim_reviews.claim: c1 = 0.6046 computed in FP64, not interval-certified
claim_reviews.evidence: GPT-5.2 correctly noted that c1 is FP64 while other constants are arb-certified. Added note that the 6x margin tolerates ~20% error in c1 (1.2^3.57 = 1.9x shift in D0).

claim_reviews.claim: MOW matching AI-assisted, needs independent verification
claim_reviews.verdict: VERIFIED — correctly identified as key remaining gap
claim_reviews.evidence: GPT-5.2 noted Remark 9 as the load-bearing admission. This is already flagged in the paper's abstract and in the MOW matching remark itself.
claim_reviews.concerns: This is the principal remaining step. Already acknowledged.

claim_reviews.claim: Paper is still a draft (Section ??, AI-review language)
claim_reviews.evidence: No 'Section ??' found in current version (may have been in an older PDF). The AI-review language and no-journal-submission stance are intentional, not oversights.

overall_assessment: GPT-5.2 provided the most detailed line-by-line review yet. Found one real proof error (transitivity |H| bound — fixed), one valid concern (c1 FP64 — acknowledged with tolerance analysis), one false alarm (D0 arithmetic — LaTeX misread, clarified), and correctly identified the MOW matching as the key remaining gap. Summary quote: 'stronger than hype, but weaker than closure' — accurate.
recommendations: FIXED: Transitivity proof step (iii) rewritten with correct Dickson argument
FIXED: D0 LaTeX clarified to prevent misreading of exponent
ACKNOWLEDGED: c1 FP64 with tolerance analysis (6x margin handles 20% error)
ALREADY KNOWN: MOW matching needs independent expert verification
certification_justification: Third independent AI review. Found and fixed a real proof error. All three reviews (Claude, Grok, GPT-5.2) converge on ACCEPT WITH REVISION, SILVER. The proof framework is mathematically sound pending MOW expert verification.
